summary: Two material risks remain: the new Ecosystem tab can still crash on structurally incomplete status data, and rejecting a `reapply-failed` entry mutates rule autonomy as if the human had rejected a fresh proposal.

findings.title: Ecosystem tab dereferences `sweep.failed.length` without validating the log entry shape, so JSON-valid but incomplete status data will crash the UI
findings.recommendation: Defensively normalize `/api/ecosystem-status` responses before rendering, or guard every access with array checks (for example `const failed = Array.isArray(sweep?.failed) ? sweep.failed : []`). `doEcosystemStatus()` currently returns the last JSON array element verbatim, so `{}` or other partial entries from a valid log file are enough to trigger `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`.

findings.title: Dismissing a `reapply-failed` entry via Reject also records a rule-level rejection, which can demote `auto` or poison promotion stats for an already-applied proposal
findings.recommendation: Split “dismiss stuck reapply failure” from “human rejects a pending proposal”, or at minimum skip `applyOutcome(..., "reject")` when the current status is `reapply-failed`. As written, the newly enabled Reject button does more than clear the queue item: it changes future autonomy behavior for that rule even though no new proposal was actually rejected.
